metadata.title: Digital Transformation Strategy: Meridian Retail Group
metadata.author: Strategy & Corporate Finance Practice
metadata.description: McKinsey-style corporate strategy assessment for Meridian Retail Group's $1.4B digital transformation initiative

elements.content: Meridian Retail Group | Board Strategy Review | March 2026

slides.speaker_notes: Good morning. Today we present our findings from a 12-week diagnostic of Meridian's digital readiness and a recommended transformation roadmap that captures $340M in incremental EBITDA by 2029.

elements.content: Meridian Retail Group faces an inflection point: digital-native competitors have captured 23% of addressable market share in 3 years. Our diagnostic reveals $340M in untapped EBITDA through three pillars: (1) unified omnichannel platform replacing 14 legacy systems, (2) AI-driven demand forecasting reducing inventory waste by 31%, and (3) personalization engine lifting conversion rates from 2.1% to 4.8%. Total investment: $1.4B over 3 years. Payback: 2.7 years. Risk-adjusted IRR: 28%.

slides.speaker_notes: The key insight from our diagnostic: Meridian is not behind in digital -- it is fragmented. The 14 disconnected systems create more data loss than any competitor advantage. Unifying them unlocks the $340M.

items: E-commerce penetration in retail reached 38% in 2025, up from 21% in 2020 -- Meridian's digital share is 14%
Digital-native competitors (Shein, Temu, Amazon) grew 3.2x faster than traditional retail in FY2025
Consumer expectation gap: 78% of Meridian's target demographic expects same-day delivery; Meridian offers 3-5 day
Store traffic declined 12% YoY but basket size increased 8% -- physical retail is shifting to experiential
McKinsey Digital Quotient assessment scores Meridian at 34/100 vs. industry median of 52/100
Top-quartile digital retailers achieve 2.4x higher EBITDA margins than bottom-quartile peers

slides.speaker_notes: The data is unambiguous. Meridian's digital gap is not a future risk -- it is a current P&L drag. The 24-point DQ gap translates directly to the margin differential we see in the financials.

elements.content: Revenue Trends: Digital vs. Traditional Channels

slides.speaker_notes: Traditional revenue is declining at 5-6% annually while digital channels grow 25-30%. The crossover point -- where digital exceeds traditional -- arrives in FY2029 at current trajectories. The question is whether Meridian leads that transition or is disrupted by it.

items: 14 disconnected systems across channels
48-hour inventory visibility lag
2.1% online conversion rate
3-5 day delivery standard
Manual markdown optimization
Siloed customer data (no single view)
12-week product launch cycle
EBITDA margin: 8.2%
Unified commerce platform (single codebase)
Real-time inventory across all nodes
4.8% projected conversion rate (+129%)
Same-day / next-day delivery (85% coverage)
AI-driven dynamic pricing (3.2% margin lift)
360-degree customer profile with CDP
4-week product launch cycle (3x faster)
Target EBITDA margin: 12.6% (+440bps)

slides.speaker_notes: Every metric improves under the digital-first model. The 440 basis point margin expansion is the primary financial justification. But the speed advantage -- 4-week vs 12-week launch cycles -- is what creates sustainable competitive advantage.

items.title: Foundation
items.description: Unified commerce platform selection and integration. CDP deployment. Cloud migration for 6 core systems. Investment: $420M.

items.title: Acceleration
items.description: AI demand forecasting rollout (31% waste reduction). Personalization engine launch. Same-day delivery in top 40 markets. Investment: $520M.

items.title: Scale & Optimize
items.description: Full omnichannel integration. Dynamic pricing across all channels. Autonomous store operations pilot. Investment: $460M.

items.title: Payback
items.description: Cumulative EBITDA uplift exceeds $1.4B investment. Run-rate incremental EBITDA: $340M/year. Digital revenue share: 52%.

slides.speaker_notes: The 3-year horizon is aggressive but achievable. The Foundation phase is the highest-risk period -- platform selection and legacy decommissioning. We recommend a parallel-run approach rather than big-bang migration.

items: Platform migration disrupts holiday season (probability: 25%, impact: $180M revenue at risk)
Talent gap: need 340 digital roles, market is tight (probability: 60%, impact: 6-month delay)
Change resistance from 12,000 store associates (probability: 70%, impact: adoption lag)
Vendor lock-in with unified platform (probability: 40%, impact: future flexibility)
Competitor acceleration during transition (probability: 50%, impact: market share)
Freeze major migrations during Q4; parallel-run approach with instant rollback capability
Acqui-hire 2 digital agencies ($40M); partner with Infosys for 200 managed resources
Invest $28M in change management; gamified training program; 500 digital champions
Microservices architecture with abstraction layers; negotiate exit clauses in vendor contracts
War room with weekly competitive intelligence; pre-build 6-month feature pipeline buffer

slides.speaker_notes: The talent risk is the one I lose sleep over. Every Fortune 500 is competing for the same digital talent. The acqui-hire strategy is unconventional but gives us 150 experienced digital builders on day one.
Note that our Year 3 targets exceed top-quartile benchmarks on 5 of 8 KPIs. This is intentional -- catching up to the median is insufficient. Meridian needs to leapfrog to earn back market share.

items: APPROVE the $1.4B, 3-year digital transformation program with phased funding gates at each 12-month milestone
APPOINT a Chief Digital Officer with P&L authority reporting directly to the CEO -- not buried under CTO or CMO
EXECUTE the acqui-hire of two digital agencies (Pixelshift and DataCraft) for $40M combined to close the talent gap immediately
RESTRUCTURE the technology organization from project-based to product-based teams aligned with customer journeys
ESTABLISH a Digital Transformation Office (DTO) with quarterly board reporting cadence and kill criteria at each gate
PARTNER with a strategic systems integrator (recommended: Infosys) for 200 managed resources over 18 months

slides.speaker_notes: Six clear actions requiring board approval today. The CDO appointment is the single most important decision -- without executive ownership at the right level, transformation programs fail 70% of the time.

elements.content:
The market will not wait. Every quarter of delay costs Meridian approximately $28M in unrealized EBITDA and 0.8 points of market share. We recommend immediate approval of Phase 1 funding ($420M) with a Q2 2026 start date.

Strategy & Corporate Finance Practice | Confidential

slides.speaker_notes: Thank you. The urgency is real but the opportunity is larger than the risk. We are happy to take questions on any aspect of the analysis, timeline, or financial model.
